idea-plugin.name: MockServer
idea-plugin.description:
MockServer lets you easily mock any system you integrate with via HTTP or HTTPS (services, REST or RPC APIs, and more), so you can develop and test against simulated dependencies instead of real ones. This plugin brings the full MockServer toolset into IntelliJ IDEA and all other JetBrains IDEs.

Features

  • Expectation file validation — name a file *.mockserver.json (or *.mockserver.jsonc) and the IDE validates it as you type, with autocompletion and hover documentation, using the same JSON Schema MockServer validates against. A single expectation object or an array of expectations (initialization JSON) is accepted.
  • Open MockServer Dashboard — opens the live dashboard in your default browser to watch requests, inspect match results, and review active expectations.
  • Open MockServer Dashboard in IDE — embeds the live dashboard inside the IDE in a dedicated right-hand tool window using the bundled JCEF (Chromium) engine, with Reload and Open-in-Browser toolbar buttons. Falls back to the external browser when JCEF is unavailable.
  • Start MockServer (Docker) — launches the configured mockserver/mockserver container on the configured port with a single click. Docker must be running; the image defaults to the plugin's own version.
  • Load Expectations Into Running Server — sends the active editor file (a single expectation or an array) to PUT /mockserver/expectation on the running server.
  • Save Recorded Expectations — retrieves expectations MockServer recorded from proxied/forwarded traffic and opens them as a .mockserver.json file in a new tab.
  • Generate Expectations From OpenAPI Spec — sends the active editor's OpenAPI/Swagger spec (JSON or YAML) to MockServer and opens the generated expectations in a new tab.
  • Send Test Request — sends the ad-hoc HTTP request described in the active editor ({ "method", "path", "headers"?, "body"? }) to the running MockServer and opens the response in a new tab.
  • Show Drift Report — fetches MockServer's mock-drift records (how real upstream responses differ from your stub expectations) and opens a formatted text report in a new tab.
  • Find Requests by Trace — enter a W3C trace id (32 hex) or a full traceparent header value; the plugin retrieves all received requests, filters those carrying a matching traceparent header, and opens the results as JSON in a new tab.
  • Upload WASM Module — picks a compiled .wasm custom-rule module with the IDE file chooser and uploads it to MockServer; the module can then be referenced by name as a WASM body matcher in an expectation ({ "type": "WASM", "moduleName": "<name>" }).
  • List WASM Modules — fetches and displays the WASM custom-rule modules registered on the running server.
  • Reset MockServer — clears all expectations and recorded logs on the running server (confirms before acting).
  • MockServer tool window — a dockable panel in the bottom tool-window bar that surfaces every action as a one-click button, grouped into Server, Editor actions, and WASM rows, so the full action set is always within reach without opening the Tools menu.
  • Settings — configure the Docker image, container name, and port under Settings | Tools | MockServer.

Quick start

  1. Create a file named my-stubs.mockserver.json — validation and completion start immediately.
  2. Run Tools > MockServer > Start MockServer (Docker) to launch a container (requires Docker).
  3. Run Load Expectations Into Running Server to push your expectations to the server.
  4. Run Open MockServer Dashboard in IDE to watch and inspect live traffic.

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3+ or any JetBrains IDE on IntelliJ Platform build 243+
  • Docker is required only for Start MockServer (Docker); all other actions connect to any MockServer already running at the configured port.
  • WASM actions require wasmEnabled=true on the running MockServer.

Full documentation — creating expectations, verifying requests, the REST API, and client libraries for Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust and PHP — is at www.mock-server.com.
idea-plugin.change-notes: • 7.1.0 — Full IDE integration: expectation-file validation with JSON Schema completion; Start MockServer via Docker; Open MockServer Dashboard in IDE (JCEF-embedded) or browser; Load Expectations, Save Recorded Expectations, Generate From OpenAPI Spec, Send Test Request; Show Drift Report; Find Requests by Trace (W3C traceparent); Upload and List WASM custom-rule modules; Reset; dockable MockServer tool window with one-click buttons; Settings (image, container, port). UX polish: a MockServer marketplace icon, per-action icons and Server / Editor / WASM grouping in the Tools menu, a status line and bold section headers in the tool window, and a friendly "no server running" panel in the embedded dashboard when MockServer is unreachable.

action.description: Opens the MockServer dashboard in your default browser
Shows the live MockServer dashboard inside the IDE using the bundled Chromium engine
Starts a MockServer container via Docker
Clears all expectations and logs on the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/reset)
Sends the active editor file to PUT /mockserver/expectation on the running MockServer
Retrieves expectations recorded from proxied traffic and opens them in a new JSON tab
Sends the active editor's OpenAPI/Swagger spec to MockServer and opens the generated expectations
Sends the active editor's ad-hoc HTTP request to the running MockServer and opens the response
Fetches MockServer's mock-drift records and opens a readable text report in a new tab
Finds every received request belonging to a W3C trace id (or traceparent) and opens them in a new JSON tab
Uploads a compiled .wasm custom-rule module to the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/wasm/modules)
Lists the WASM custom-rule modules registered on the running MockServer (GET /mockserver/wasm/modules)
